'''Sufar''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Unidentified Corellian colony|colony world]]. An outlaw, he was exiled from his home in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] for committing several [[Death/Legends|murders]]. On the run from [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], he was rescued by [[Qulok's Fist]], a small band of criminals whose operations included [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunting, [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]], [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]], and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] work. After killing the hunters who had pursued him, Sufar and Qulok's Fist operated throughout the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]], and the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. The group worked occasionally for [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], the Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] of the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]], and [[Skirmish at Shintel|engaged]] the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' in [[Space warfare/Legends|battle]] in the [[Shintel system/Legends|Shintel system]] at his request.

==Biography==
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Sufar was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] and raised on a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Unidentified Corellian colony|colony world]]. He became an outlaw, believing that the only way he could continue living was to [[Death/Legends|kill]] any potential threats to himself. Around [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], he was exiled from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] following a string of murders he committed. A [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] was placed on Sufar, and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] attempted to collect on it. He was rescued by [[Qulok's Fist]], a small group of criminals and bounty hunters that operated within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], whose membership included [[Kolig]], [[Padak]], and [[Thanis Gul-Rah]]. Sufar joined them, and together they captured the hunters that were after him; the Corellian subsequently [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] them to death.<ref name="tdc" />

Sufar and Qulok's Fist conducted operations throughout the Outer Rim Territories, eventually moving into the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]] and then the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. In the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]], the group was employed by Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]],<ref name="tdc" /> who had declared himself a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] during the fragmentation of the Empire following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="tsb">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> Sufar was also involved in a hunt on a backwater world that devolved into a massacre of the local population. This act caused Thanis Gul-Rah to retire from the gang and take up residence on [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], the sector [[Capital/Legends|capital]].<ref name="tdc" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' takes place four [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which equates to [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> Kal'Shebbol was [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberated]] by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic task force (Kal'Shebbol)|forces]]. Although they managed to secure the planet, Sarne escaped with the majority of his [[Moff Sarne's forces|fleet]] into the Kathol sector.<ref name="tsb" /> Learning that the New Republic had tasked the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' to [[DarkStryder Campaign|hunt him down]], Sarne contacted Qulok's Fist and employed their services in shadowing the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] and reporting back to him on its activities.<ref name="tdc" />

Several [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] into the assignment, Sarne again contacted Qulok's Fist and relayed a scheme he had devised to stage an assault on the ''FarStar'' in the [[Shintel system/Legends|Shintel system]].<ref name="sd">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Shintel Downtime|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> The [[Corvette/Legends|corvette's]] command crew had been fed false information regarding Sarne's whereabouts by [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Olaver Lansel]], a deep-cover [[Agent/Legends|agent]] on the planet [[Gandle Ott]].<ref name="dirp">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Death Is Remotely Possible|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> The information led them to [[Shintel]], where Sarne had previously established a [[Imperial Depot 4290-1|supply depot]], which he had subsequently abandoned in his flight into the Kathol sector. There, another deep-cover agent. [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Palme]], uploaded a virus into the corvette's [[Computer/Legends|computer]] systems. When the New Republic vessel attempted to jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], the virus caused the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] engines to overload. Sufar crewed the ''[[Steel Fist]]'', Qulok Fist's main spacecraft, during a [[Skirmish at Shintel|brief engagement]] with the ''FarStar'' before the gang withdrew from combat<ref name="sd" /> since Sarne only wished to harass the New Republic vessel. The warlord planned to use the ''FarStar'' as bait for a trap he was setting to lure a large New Republic force into a battle of his design that would have allowed him to ultimately destroy any opposition and allow him to retake control of the Kathol sector unopposed.<ref name="ko">''[[The Kathol Outback]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Sufar was a cold-[[Blood/Legends|blooded]] murderer, though he was subtle and sophisticated. He lacked a sense of honor, and had a savage air about him. Sufar lived to kill, believing it was the only way he could survive. He constantly evaluated anyone as a potential target, determining their strengths and weaknesses. To him, everyone was a potential threat to be eliminated. The other members of Qulok's Fist were the only individuals that Sufar remotely considered to be friends. He had [[Color/Legends|blond]] hair that he kept cut short and light skin.<ref name="tdc" />

Sufar employed a wide range of techniques to kill his victims. Preferring to act behind the scenes rather than expose himself to a direct confrontation, he used [[Poison/Legends|poisons]] and [[Bomb/Legends|bombs]] to kill his targets, as well as high-tech solutions such as [[Slicer/Legends|slicing]] computer systems to arrange various accidents. He was trained to use [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], and was skilled in [[Unarmed combat/Legends|hand-to-hand]] and [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee]] combat. Sufar could pilot [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles, and [[Gunner/Legends|operate]] weapons systems on smaller starships. He was also trained in first aid. Sufar armed himself with a [[Blaster carbine/Legends|blaster carbine]], and carried a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]] and a [[Knife/Legends|knife]] for additional defense. He also used [[Detonite/Legends|detonite]] with [[timer fuse]]s, and employed a [[security override kit]] when necessary.<ref name="tdc" />

==Behind the scenes==
Sufar was created for ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', a supplement for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' by [[West End Games]]. Sufar appeared in the "Rogues Gallery" section written by [[Peter M. Schweighofer]], and was included so the Gamemaster could use him—and Qulok's Fist—in an original adventure. Qulok's Fist would go on to appear in the adventure ''[[Shintel Downtime]]'', although they would only be encountered aboard their ship, the ''Steel Fist''.
